seoTitle: How to Make Candlesticks Bigger and Bolder on TradingView
metaDescription: Thin candlesticks hard to read? Learn how to enlarge candle bodies, adjust borders, and pick better color contrast on TradingView for clearer charts.
intro: Thin, small candlesticks can make it harder to read price action, especially when you're layering on indicators like RSI, MACD, or moving averages. This guide covers increasing candle body thickness, adjusting border lines, and choosing color contrasts that work for both day and night chart themes. The same customization works whether you're on TradingView's desktop or mobile app.

steps.heading: Open chart appearance settings
steps.body: Access the chart settings menu where TradingView's candlestick styling options live.

steps.heading: Increase candle body thickness
steps.body: Adjust the body width setting so each candlestick appears larger and easier to read at a glance.

steps.heading: Adjust the border lines
steps.body: Fine-tune the border thickness and color around each candle to make individual candles stand out more clearly.

steps.heading: Choose better color contrast
steps.body: Pick colors that contrast well with your chart's background, whether you're using a light or dark theme, so bullish and bearish candles are easy to tell apart.

steps.heading: Apply the same settings on mobile
steps.body: If you also trade from your phone, repeat the adjustments in the mobile app so your charts look consistent across devices.

faq.question: Why do my candlesticks look too thin on TradingView?
faq.answer: Thin candles are usually just the default sizing, which can be adjusted through the chart's appearance settings to make them wider and bolder.

faq.question: Does making candles bigger affect indicator accuracy?
faq.answer: No, enlarging candlesticks is purely a visual adjustment - it makes price action easier to read but doesn't change how indicators like RSI or MACD calculate.

faq.question: Can I make candles bigger on the TradingView mobile app?
faq.answer: Yes, similar candlestick sizing and color options are available in the mobile app, though the menu layout differs slightly from desktop.

faq.question: What color contrast works best for reading candlesticks at night?
faq.answer: Brighter, higher-contrast colors against a dark chart background tend to be easier to distinguish, which is worth adjusting separately from your day theme.
